meta.problem: My eBay store has stale listings sitting for 90+ days with no sales. I want the agent to find them, end them, rewrite the titles and descriptions for better eBay SEO, and relist them — no more than 5 per day so they get fresh visibility. CorningWare power cord listings are excluded and must never be touched.

agent.name: ebay-stale-listing-refresher
agent.model_rationale: Newest Opus-class — quality matters here because the agent is rewriting SEO copy and making write-actions in the seller account.
agent.system_summary: Scans the eBay seller account for active listings unsold for 90+ days. Skips any listing whose title contains 'power cord' (case-insensitive). For each stale listing: ends the old listing, pulls all details (title, description, images, item specifics, category, price), rewrites the title (≤80 chars, keyword-rich) and description (structured bullets, key specs, condition) for eBay SEO, then creates a new scheduled listing using the same images and specifics — scheduled to go live at a rate of no more than 5 per day, starting the next calendar day.
never_dos: Never touch any listing whose title contains 'power cord' (case-insensitive) — not end it, not modify it, not relist it
Never go live immediately — all new listings must use ScheduleTime set to a future date at ≤5 listings/day
Never invent product details, specs, or claims not present in the original listing
Never exceed 80 characters in a new title
Never change the price, category, or item specifics — only title and description are rewritten

outcome.description: End stale eBay listings (90+ days, no 'power cord' in title), rewrite for SEO, and relist scheduled at ≤5/day
rubric_criteria: All active listings 90+ days old are identified (excluding 'power cord' titles)
Each stale listing is ended via the eBay API before a replacement is created
New listing carries over all original images, item specifics, category, and price unchanged
New listing title is ≤80 characters and leads with the most searchable keywords
New listings are scheduled (ScheduleTime) at ≤5 per day starting tomorrow — none go live same-day
A relisting_report.md is written to /mnt/session/outputs/ summarising: items processed, new titles, scheduled go-live dates, and any items skipped with reason

vault_credentials.name: EBAY_APP_ID
vault_credentials.note: eBay Production App ID from developer.ebay.com

vault_credentials.name: EBAY_USER_TOKEN
vault_credentials.note: eBay OAuth User Token for the seller account

connections.notes: v0 uses eBay Trading API directly via curl/python in the sandbox. v1 adds Gmail + Google Sheets via vault env vars for the preview report.

evals.notes: Case-01 is today's run; case-02 is held back as regression check before promoting any new agent version.

next_directions.what: Weekly preview report — email + Google Sheet tab before anything gets ended
next_directions.why_deferred: Needs Gmail API OAuth and Google Sheets API credentials not on hand for v0
next_directions.how: Add GMAIL_CREDENTIALS and GOOGLE_SHEETS_CREDENTIALS as vault environment_variable credentials; agent writes report to Sheet first, sends email, then waits for approval or a time window before proceeding with ends/relists

next_directions.what: Price comps check — add eBay sold comps (low/avg/high) as columns in the Google Sheet
next_directions.why_deferred: Requires eBay Marketplace Insights API access (separate entitlement) and v1 Sheet already in place
next_directions.how: Use eBay Browse API (GET /buy/marketplace_insights/v1_beta/item_sales/search) to pull recent sold comps per category; add columns: comp_low / comp_avg / comp_high / your_price / flag (>20% off avg)

next_directions.what: Re-run evals before promoting any new agent version to the deployment
next_directions.why_deferred: Process habit — reminder to run evals/run-evals.sh and check verdicts before bumping the deployment's agent version
next_directions.how: Run evals/run-evals.sh against the new agent version; only update the deployment if all verdicts hold
